LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TRAVERLERS CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF INSURANCE CONTRACT AND BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING ASSERTED BY PLAINTIFF MOHAMED EDAIS DBA BIG SAVE MARKET, MUSTAFA "STEVE" EDAIS, AND THE JJ FISH & CHICKEN ENTITIES IN PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFFS HAVE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED THE LEGAL EFFECT OF THE CONTRACTS IT CONTENDS WERE BREACHED BY TRAVELERS. PLAINTIFFS HAVE ALSO ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS THAT TRAVELERS DENIED THEIR INSURANCE CLAIM IN BAD FAITH AND WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT TO FILE ITS RESPONSIVE PLEADING NO LATER THAN DEC. 18, 2017. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER THAT REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: NEWTON LAM; CLERK: G. GONZALES; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR#6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Travelers Casualty Insurance Company Of Americas Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-01-2018 Set For Trial On Dec-03-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-15-2018 Set For Trial On Sep-24-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Mar-01-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Continued To Feb-28-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Carla V. Dos Santos, Esq. of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara LLP, Tel. 510.540.4881, for defendant and moving party;
Yosef Peretz, Esq. of Peretz & Associates, Tel. 415.732.3777 ext.102, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT RICK BARTHOLOMEW dba Safe & Sound Systems' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings as to the first, second, and third causes of action in plaintiffs' complaint for breach of contract asserted by Mohamed Edais, breach of contractual obligations owed to third-party beneficiaries and negligence asserted by all plaintiffs is granted in its entirety. The contractual provision that shortened the statute of limitation period to one year is reasonable and therefore, enforceable. Assuming without deciding that the contract has some modicum of procedural unconscionability, the contract is nonetheless enforceable because it is not substantively unconscionable since it is neither overly harsh nor one-sided. The provision is also applicable to plaintiffs' negligence cause of action because it does not exculpate Safe & Sound from negligence liability, but rather, reasonably shortens the statute of limitation period to one-year for such claims. Plaintiffs alleging breach of contract as third-party beneficiaries are also barred by the one-year time period because they are as much subject to statute of limitations as the promisee to the contract that created the right of the beneficiary. Plaintiffs' causes of action began to accrue on August 28, 2015 when Big Save Market was burglarized and plaintiffs untimely filed the action two years later on August 24, 2017. Accordingly, the causes of action are barred by the one-year time limitation. Safe & Sounds request for judicial notice is granted.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Edward P. Murphy, Esq. of Foran Glennon, Tel. 510.740.1500, for defendant and moving party;
Yosef Peretz, Esq. of Peretz & Associates, Tel. 415.732.3777 ext.102, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TRAVERLERS CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER to the fourth and fifth causes of action for breach of insurance contract and breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing asserted by Plaintiff Mohamed Edais dba Big Save Market, Mustafa "Steve" Edais, and the JJ Fish & Chicken Entities in plaintiffs' complaint is OVERRULED in its entirety. Plaintiffs have adequately alleged the legal effect of the contracts it contends were breached by Travelers. Plaintiffs have also alleged sufficient facts that Travelers denied their insurance claim in bad faith and without good cause. Plaintiffs' request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant to file its responsive pleading no later than Dec. 18, 2017.

Counsel for Plaintiffs to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org.
